vocab.example: I love using floor-to-ceiling mirrors to create a spatial illusion, making my cramped apartment feel twice its actual size.

vocab.meaning: khung mạ vàng chạm trổ công phu
vocab.example: To add a touch of vintage elegance, I would hang a large mirror with an ornate gilded frame above the fireplace.

vocab.meaning: tăng cường ánh sáng tự nhiên
vocab.example: Strategically placing a mirror opposite the window helps to amplify natural light and brighten up the darker corners of the room.
I prefer frameless, circular mirrors because they align perfectly with the minimalist aesthetic I want for my bedroom.
A sunburst mirror can serve as a stunning focal point in a living room, immediately drawing the guest's attention.

vocab.meaning: làm rối mắt/choán hết tầm nhìn
vocab.example: I avoid overusing mirrors because too many reflections can clutter the visual field and make a space feel chaotic.

vocab.meaning: các cạnh được mài vát/vát xéo
vocab.example: Mirrors with beveled edges add a sophisticated, multi-dimensional look to the wall without needing a bulky frame.

vocab.meaning: phối cảnh/sơ đồ thiết kế nội thất
vocab.example: Incorporating metallic-tinted mirrors into my interior design scheme adds a contemporary and chic vibe to the house.

vocab.meaning: sự sắp xếp theo hình khối địa chất/hình học
vocab.example: I’m planning to create a gallery wall using a geometric arrangement of small hexagonal mirrors.

vocab.meaning: chiều sâu và sự đa chiều
vocab.example: Using mirrors is a classic trick to add depth and dimension to otherwise flat and lifeless walls.
